violations.text: Establishment's Rhode Island Manager Certified In Food Safety license is expired. FMC must re-instate. PIC has current accredited manager food safety certificate. Application provided during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025

The doors (A1, B3 and D8) to the outside, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
Establishment's Rhode Island Manager Certified In Food Safety license is expired. FMC must re-instate. Application provided during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025
Laminate lined shelving in dry storage area observed in poor repair (exposed composite wood and taped). Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2026

Chickpea Salad was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cold holding unit was observed at ambient temperatures of 32-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Per staff chickpea salad was placed into cold holding unit recently. Chickpea salad was placed into freezer to blast chill. Cold holding unit was covered to allow cold temperature to distribute within unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the dry storage room shelves have an accumulation of residue, old pest excrement and other debris. PIC informed area to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored on tray rack. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2025

Pasta salad was observed held at 67 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, pasta salad was recently composed. Pasta salad was placed into shallow pan in a thin layer in freezer to blast chill. Pasta salad was chilled to 39 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024
The 2-door reach-in refrigerator observed with ice build-up on the condenser. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/14/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/13/2023
The door to the Vulcan hot holding oven observed in poor repair. Door shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
The condensate from the 2 door freezer unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. -Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the can opener during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023

A few droppings observed in the tray storage shelves. Per interview of staff, pest control is active. -Establishment shall monitor droppings. If droppings still observed after cleaning the area, establishment shall get pest control in to do an assessment/evaluation of facility and schedule for more frequent service until no droppings observed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023
